proposed_hypotheses.text: Among all patients in the cohort, the response rate (objective_response) is higher in patients who received treatment_pembrolizumab than in those who did not.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate 0.1739 on pembrolizumab vs 0.1640 off (absolute difference +0.0099, chi-square p=0.00329).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across the entire cohort (most of whom are KRAS G12C negative), the marginal response rate is higher in patients receiving treatment_sotorasib than in those who do not.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate 0.1695 on sotorasib vs 0.1687 off (absolute difference +0.0008, chi-square p=0.82).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across the entire cohort, the marginal response rate differs between patients who received treatment_osimertinib and those who did not.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate 0.1655 on osimertinib vs 0.1704 off (absolute difference -0.0050, chi-square p=0.175).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across the entire cohort, the marginal response rate differs between patients who received treatment_olaparib and those who did not.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate 0.1699 on olaparib vs 0.1685 off (absolute difference +0.0013, chi-square p=0.714).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the kras_g12c-positive subgroup, treatment_sotorasib increases the objective_response rate substantially compared to no sotorasib.

There is a positive interaction between treatment_sotorasib and kras_g12c on objective_response: sotorasib's benefit on response is larger in kras_g12c-positive patients than in kras_g12c-negative patients.

analyses.code: Within KRAS G12C+: response rate sotorasib vs no-sotorasib
analyses.result_summary: In kras_g12c+ (n=6368): response rate 0.175 on sotorasib vs 0.166 off (delta +0.008, p=0.41). In kras_g12c- (n=43632): 0.169 vs 0.169 (delta -0.000, p=0.943).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the egfr_mutation-positive subgroup, treatment_osimertinib increases the objective_response rate substantially compared with no osimertinib.
There is a positive interaction between treatment_osimertinib and egfr_mutation on objective_response: osimertinib's effect is larger in EGFR-mutant patients than in EGFR-wildtype patients.

analyses.code: Within EGFR+: rate osimertinib vs no-osimertinib
analyses.result_summary: In egfr_mutation+ (n=6517): 0.164 on osimertinib vs 0.166 off (delta -0.002, p=0.841). In egfr_mutation- (n=43483): 0.166 vs 0.171 (delta -0.005, p=0.17).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the brca2_mutation-positive subgroup, treatment_olaparib increases the objective_response rate compared with no olaparib.
There is a positive interaction between treatment_olaparib and brca2_mutation on objective_response: olaparib's benefit is larger in BRCA2-mutant patients than in BRCA2-wildtype patients.

analyses.code: Within BRCA2+: rate olaparib vs no-olaparib
analyses.result_summary: In brca2_mutation+ (n=1519): 0.146 on olaparib vs 0.168 off (delta -0.022, p=0.308). In brca2_mutation- (n=48481): 0.171 vs 0.169 (delta +0.002, p=0.591).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher pdl1_tps is associated with higher objective_response rate among patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.
There is a positive interaction between treatment_pembrolizumab and pdl1_tps on objective_response: pembrolizumab's benefit on response is larger in patients with higher pdl1_tps.

analyses.result_summary: Main pdl1_tps log-odds = -0.137 (p=0.0904); pembro x pdl1_tps interaction log-odds = +0.591 (p=1.57e-07). PD-L1>=0.5 stratum (n=10749): 0.209 on pembro vs 0.156 off (delta +0.054, p=6.45e-13). PD-L1<0.5 stratum (n=39251): 0.164 vs 0.166 (delta -0.002, p=0.583).

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_pembrolizumab and tmb_high on objective_response: pembrolizumab's benefit on response is larger in patients with tmb_high than in tmb_high-negative patients.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction log-odds pembro*tmb_high = +0.184 (p=0.000415). TMB-high stratum (n=14258): 0.197 on pembro vs 0.167 off (delta +0.030, p=4.86e-06). TMB-low stratum (n=35742): 0.165 vs 0.163 (delta +0.002, p=0.602).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with stk11_mutation derive less benefit from treatment_pembrolizumab than stk11_mutation-negative patients on objective_response (negative interaction between pembrolizumab and stk11_mutation).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction log-odds pembro*stk11 = -0.211 (p=0.00178). STK11+ stratum (n=7451): 0.158 on pembro vs 0.173 off (delta -0.015, p=0.0781). STK11- stratum (n=42549): 0.177 vs 0.162 (delta +0.014).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps (worse performance status) is associated with lower objective_response rates across the cohort.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response rate (better baseline nutrition/inflammation status predicts response).
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower objective_response rate (high LDH is a marker of disease burden/aggressiveness).
Higher nlr (neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with lower objective_response rate.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response rate.
Patients with has_brain_mets have a lower objective_response rate than patients without brain metastases.
Patients with liver_mets have a lower objective_response rate than patients without liver metastases.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate 0.1436 with brain mets vs 0.1775 without (delta -0.0339, p=1.76e-18).
Response rate 0.1728 with liver mets vs 0.1683 without (delta +0.0046, p=0.331).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Never-smokers (smoking_status == 'never') derive less benefit from treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response than ever-smokers (negative interaction between pembrolizumab and never-smoker status).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction log-odds pembro*never_smoker = -0.072 (p=0.289). Never-smoker stratum (n=7459): 0.164 on pembro vs 0.163 off (delta +0.001). Ever-smoker stratum (n=42541): 0.176 vs 0.164 (delta +0.011).

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is an interaction between treatment_pembrolizumab and histology=='squamous' on objective_response.
Patients with stage_iv have a lower objective_response rate than patients without stage IV disease.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate 0.1540 stage IV vs 0.1964 non-stage IV (delta -0.0425, p=9.53e-34).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The objective_response rate differs across race_ethnicity categories.
The objective_response rate differs across insurance_type categories.

analyses.result_summary: Response rates by insurance_type: {'medicaid': 0.16130875839405628, 'medicare': 0.16847672112569648, 'private': 0.17202239822428492, 'uninsured': 0.17}; chi-square p=0.232.

proposed_hypotheses.text: EGFR-mutant patients (egfr_mutation==1) derive less benefit from treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response than EGFR-wildtype patients (negative interaction).
Higher fatigue_grade is associated with lower objective_response rate.
Higher pain_nrs is associated with lower objective_response rate.
Higher dyspnea_grade is associated with lower objective_response rate.

After adjustment for demographics, ECOG, stage, metastatic burden, laboratory and biomarker covariates, none of the four treatments (pembrolizumab, sotorasib, olaparib, osimertinib) shows a marginal (non-interaction) effect on objective_response distinguishable from zero.

analyses.code: Multivariable Logit with all four treatments + clinical covariates
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted main-effect log-odds (no interactions): pembrolizumab=+0.076 (p=0.00159); sotorasib=+0.005 (p=0.833); olaparib=+0.012 (p=0.645); osimertinib=-0.035 (p=0.179).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for clinical covariates and treatment main effects, the treatment_sotorasib × kras_g12c interaction has a positive coefficient on the log-odds of objective_response (i.e., sotorasib's benefit is concentrated in KRAS G12C+ patients).
After adjustment, the treatment_osimertinib × egfr_mutation interaction has a positive coefficient on the log-odds of objective_response.
After adjustment, the treatment_olaparib × brca2_mutation interaction has a positive coefficient on the log-odds of objective_response.
After adjustment, the treatment_pembrolizumab × pdl1_tps interaction has a positive coefficient on the log-odds of objective_response.

Among targetable oncogenic alterations (alk_fusion, braf_v600e, met_exon14_skipping, ros1_fusion, ret_fusion), at least one biomarker shows a marginally different objective_response rate vs. patients without that alteration.

analyses.code: For each biomarker: chi-square biomarker vs response

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with prior_immunotherapy have lower objective_response rates than those without prior immunotherapy.
There is a negative interaction between treatment_pembrolizumab and prior_immunotherapy on objective_response (less benefit when re-treated with IO).
At least one of the 23 germline SNPs (snp_*) is associated with objective_response after Bonferroni correction (p < 0.05 / number of SNPs tested).

analyses.code: For each snp: Logit(response ~ snp); apply Bonferroni at 0.05/n_snps
analyses.result_summary: 23 SNPs tested; Bonferroni threshold p < 0.00217. Number reaching threshold: 0. Min p across SNPs: 0.0182 (SNP=snp_rs7412, beta=+0.100).
